meta.review_note: Review Leverage counts the distinct teammates whose PRs an engineer left inline code-review comments on, over the full 90-day window (fetched via the authenticated GitHub API). It rewards the high-leverage, often-invisible work of unblocking others that raw output metrics miss.

dimensions.label: Scope & Ownership
dimensions.desc: Distinct PRs shipped in the window. Volume of self-contained, merged units of work — not lines of code.

dimensions.label: Criticality
dimensions.desc: How much of their work lands in hot, shared, core code (backend, migrations, product logic) vs. tests or generated files. Each file weighted by how many other engineers also touch it.

dimensions.label: Review Leverage
dimensions.desc: How much they unblock others through code review: the number of distinct teammates whose PRs they left inline review comments on, over the full 90 days. Reviewing broadly is high-leverage, invisible work that raw output metrics miss.

dimensions.label: Collaboration Centrality
dimensions.desc: Eigenvector centrality in the engineer co-edit network: engineers who work across the same files as many teammates sit at the center of coordination and unblock the most people.

dimensions.label: Consistency
dimensions.desc: Share of the 13 weeks in which they shipped at least one PR — sustained contribution rather than a single spike.

dimensions.label: Reliability
dimensions.desc: Did their shipped work stick? Starts at 100 and is penalised when their PRs are later reverted.
